headline: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 release drew skeptical reviews and reported bans even as agent tooling and voice infrastructure pushed forward.
body: GPT-5 dominated the week's opinion flow: AICodeKing rated the GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna variants competent but unremarkable, flagging mixed benchmarks and concerning cyber capabilities against OpenAI's safety claims, while a separate report described GPT-5.6 and Fable as banned and a summit panel cited a federal recall of Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models. Agent and workflow tooling moved in parallel, with Google Antigravity automating file handling and repository organization and Google Cloud outlining the harness components that take agents to production. Voice infrastructure advanced through the TEN Framework, which handles streaming audio, interruption handling, and collision detection for real-time agents, and Gemini Canvas extended natural-language generation into sites, dashboards, and audio. Conditional generation and observability are breaking out on the technique side, alongside new entrants like posterior collapse and latent dynamics learning.
